features.name: App Infrastructure & Logging
features.description: Handles application startup, crash/hang logging, system tray icon with notification badge counter, balloon notifications, structured app logging with rotation, safe background file writes, and global constants.

features.name: App Startup & Configuration
features.description: Bootstraps the WPF application, wires up managers and services at the composition root (MainWindow), manages app-wide constants, and handles tray icon and window lifecycle.

features.name: Feature Context Resolution
features.description: Resolves which features are relevant to a given task description using hybrid keyword+vector search, then builds a context block for prompt injection. Also handles post-completion feature updates.

features.name: Feature Initialization & Discovery
features.description: Performs initial codebase analysis to discover and create features by scanning source files and calling Claude Sonnet to identify logical features with their files, keywords, and dependencies. Excludes non-code assets (.png, .meta files) from discovery scans to focus on relevant source code.

features.name: Feature Mode Orchestration
features.description: Manages long-running iterative AI feature development cycles: multi-phase execution (TeamPlanning, PlanConsolidation, Execution, Evaluation), runtime cap enforcement, consecutive failure tracking, and token budget management.

features.name: Feature Registry
features.description: Maintains a persistent registry of codebase features with signatures, keywords, dependencies, and module membership using a database-backed storage system that reduces merge conflicts compared to the previous JSON-per-file approach. Provides feature lookup by task description or symbol name and builds context blocks for prompt injection.

features.name: Feature Update Agent
features.description: Post-completion fire-and-forget agent that uses Haiku to update the feature registry based on what a completed task changed. Keeps the registry current without blocking the task lifecycle.

features.name: File Lock Management
features.description: Coordinates exclusive file access between concurrently running tasks to prevent conflicts. Queues tasks that conflict on locked files and unblocks them when locks are released, including a Git operation guard.

features.name: Git Operations
features.description: Provides a safe, testable wrapper around Git CLI commands: running arbitrary git commands, capturing HEAD, committing with path specs, listing changed files, and retrieving file diffs for output display. Enforces safe practices via GitOperationGuard.

features.name: Task History & Persistence
features.description: Persists completed tasks to disk, loads history on startup, cleans up entries beyond retention period, and saves/restores the active task queue across app restarts.

features.name: LLM Services (Claude & Gemini)
features.description: Provides streaming and non-streaming chat interfaces to Claude (Anthropic) and Gemini (Google) APIs, including API key management, usage tracking, model selection, image generation, and structured JSON outputs via SendStructuredHaikuAsync method.

features.name: Main Window & UI Composition
features.description: The composition root of the WPF application: wires all managers, handles UI events, manages layout and panels, and coordinates top-level workflows. Updated to keep completed tasks visible in active tabs without auto-hiding, preventing session termination and enabling follow-up work without restart. MainWindow.xaml uses separate ResourceDictionary files in Controls/Templates/ for improved startup parse time.

features.name: MCP Server Integration
features.description: Manages MCP-for-Unity server connections: checks if the server process is running, monitors health with auto-restart, and manages per-project connection state. Automatically enables MCP when auto-mode is disabled in a game project.

features.name: Module Registry
features.description: Manages module-level groupings of features, persisting ModuleEntry data alongside the feature registry. Provides lookup of which features belong to which module.

features.name: Task Output Management
features.description: Creates and manages per-task output tabs in the UI, handles ANSI-stripped streaming output rendering and structured code diffs with syntax highlighting (green for additions, red for removals), supports Gemini image galleries, manages overflow when output exceeds the cap, and provides follow-up output injection.

features.name: Output Processing & Subtask Extraction
features.description: Processes raw Claude CLI output to extract subtasks, team members, and inject child outputs back into parent tasks. Handles token limit errors and model-switching on retry.

features.name: Preprocessor Skip Rules for Task Types
Project Management
features.description: Manages the set of active projects: metadata, per-project rules and instructions, display descriptions, color assignments, crash/log paths, and game-project detection. Acts as the primary source of project configuration consumed by tasks and prompts.

features.name: Prompt Building & Context Assembly
features.description: Assembles full AI prompts from modular blocks: system instructions, project rules, MCP configuration, feature context, skills, and user input. Also handles context reduction for token-limit retries and deduplication of repeated context.

features.name: Self-Improvement Engine
features.description:
New feature identified from task: Design and implement a self-improving feedback system for Spritely that:

1. **Feedback Collection**: After each task execution, automatically analyze outcomes:
   - What went well (successes, efficient solutions, good prompts)
   - What went poorly (failures, inefficiencies, misunderstandings)
   - Capture execution metrics, user feedback, and task results

2. **Automated Small Improvements**: Apply low-risk tweaks automatically:
   - Update CLAUDE.md with discovered best practices
   - Refine system prompts based on patterns
   - Adjust task parameters and workflows

3. **Task-Based Large Improvements**: Generate actionable tasks for substantial changes:
   - Architecture improvements
   - New features or system redesigns
   - Significant refactoring needs

4. **Project-Wide Application**: Extend learning to new projects:
   - Similar feedback loop for each project's workflow
   - Project-specific rule improvements
   - Knowledge sharing across projects

5. **Implementation Approach**:
   - First, investigate the current task execution pipeline, task history storage, and CLAUDE.md management
   - Design the feedback analysis engine (what metrics matter, how to score success)
   - Design the learning/update engine (when/how to modify CLAUDE.md vs create tasks)
   - Integrate into TaskExecutionManager and task completion flow
   - Test feedback loop with real tasks

Start with proper investigation of existing systems, then design and implement the full learning loop.

features.name: Semantic & Hybrid Search
features.description: Provides vector-based semantic search over features, source file chunks, and task history using Voyage AI embeddings stored in SQLite. Combines vector similarity, keyword scoring, symbol matching, and task history for hybrid relevance ranking.

features.name: Skill Auto-Discovery from Completed Tasks
features.description:
New feature identified from task: Implement automatic skill discovery from completed tasks. After a task completes successfully in TaskExecutionManager.CompleteWithVerification, launch a SkillDiscoveryAgent (fire-and-forget, similar to FeatureUpdateAgent) that:

1. Passes the task summary and a condensed excerpt of the task output to Haiku
2. Prompts Haiku to identify any generalizable technique, pattern, or reusable instruction from the completed work
3. If Haiku returns a candidate skill, surfaces a non-blocking toast notification to the user offering to save it via SkillManager

The SkillDiscoveryAgent should follow the existing FeatureUpdateAgent pattern in Managers/, use the Haiku service for analysis, and integrate seamlessly with the task completion flow without blocking task UI updates or next-task execution.

features.name: Skill Management
features.description: Manages user-defined custom instruction blocks (skills) that can be toggled on/off and injected into AI task prompts. Supports global and per-project skills with a full CRUD editor dialog.

features.name: Task Decomposition & Team Spawning
features.description: Handles automatic decomposition of complex tasks into subtasks and spawning of parallel agent teams. Parses SUBTASKS and TEAM JSON blocks from agent output, creates child tasks with dependencies, and coordinates phase completion.

features.name: Task Execution Engine
features.description: Launches, monitors, and manages the full execution lifecycle of AI tasks with proper state machine transitions: building Claude CLI commands, handling process I/O, managing pause/resume/cancel, auto-commit, subtask injection, and token limit retries. Ensures tasks transition from Queued to Stored (executing) status when actually running.

features.name: Task History & Group Tracking
features.description: Persists completed task history, active queue snapshots, and aggregated task group states. Enables session recovery and historical reporting.

features.name: Task Lifecycle & State
features.description: Defines the core AgentTask model and its full lifecycle with proper state machine transitions: status transitions (Queued → Stored → Running → Completed), runtime context, flags, and property-change notifications. Ensures mutually exclusive states tied to actual execution state. Acts as the central shared data contract for all task-related features.

features.name: Task Orchestration & DAG
features.description: Manages a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of task dependencies, ensuring tasks are started only when all their dependencies have resolved. Properly transitions queued tasks to running status when unblocked. Fires TaskReady events and detects dependency cycles.

features.name: Task Preprocessing & Flag Inference
features.description: Sends a task description to Claude Haiku before execution to infer optimal flags (feature mode, auto-decompose, spawn team, apply fix, MCP, extended planning). Applies inferred flags to the AgentTask without overriding user-set values.

features.name: Task UI & Graph Visualization
features.description: Renders task cards, output tabs with live streaming, task dependency graph, and workflow composer. Provides the primary user-facing task management interface.

features.name: Integrated Terminal
features.description: Embeds a real ConPTY-backed terminal within the application supporting multiple named tabs, full VT100 emulation, command history, working directory tracking, keyboard input handling (arrow keys, Enter, and other keypresses forwarded to the active process), and Ctrl+C interrupt.

features.name: Theming & Shared UI Components
features.description: Defines the app-wide dark theme through XAML resource dictionaries, semantic brush keys, and value converters. Provides shared dialog infrastructure (DarkDialog, DarkDialogWindow) and common UI helpers.

features.name: Tray Icon Task Completion Badge
features.description:
New feature identified from task: Add a notification badge counter to the tray icon that displays the count of finished tasks while the app is not focused. The badge should:
- Display in the top-right corner of the icon
- Show the count inside a circle
- Cap the counter at 99 (show "99" if exceeded)
- Clear/hide the badge when the app is focused (activated)
- Increment when tasks finish and the app is not focused
- Be properly rendered on the tray icon visual

features.name: UI Theming & Style System
features.description: WPF resource dictionaries defining the dark theme, semantic color brushes, and control templates for all UI elements. Ensures visual consistency across dialogs, controls, and the main window.
